reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware interpretation of gas-per-capacity vs absolute volume, SOC-dependent flammability limits, and qualitative energy-partition inference (heat vs gas), with correct choices tightly tied to those stated observations. Decoys are largely plausible and single-fault, but one option (A) bundles multiple leaps (range definition + ignition probability), and stylistic cues (“always,” “directly implies”) make a couple of eliminations somewhat heuristic for experts.

The prompt cleanly targets a single expert judgment: separating consequence (blast/TNT-equivalent) from initiation/propagation risk (activation energy, SADT sensitivity), with tight boundaries against overclaiming. However, as a SHORT_ANSWER item it has no decoy set to audit, forcing D2 low and limiting discriminative signal to rubric grading quality rather than option-level separation; neutrality is slightly weakened by the strongly leading phrasing that flags the engineer’s claim as flawed.
